name: Waiahole Poi Factory
tagline: One of the last real poi factories on Oahu — fresh poi, laulau, and squid luau in a Windward valley setting.
mustOrder: Fresh poi — hand-pounded, creamy, the real deal
Laulau — pork and fish wrapped in taro leaves, steamed until melt-tender
Squid luau — squid simmered in coconut milk and taro leaves
parking: Small gravel lot right off Kamehameha Hwy. Easy to spot.
talkStory: Waiahole Poi Factory is a cultural treasure. They've been making poi here in the valley for generations — this is one of the last places on Oahu where you can get fresh poi made the traditional way. The setting is beautiful — lush Windward valley with the Koolau mountains right there. Open limited hours (usually weekends and some weekdays), so check before you go. This is real Hawaiian food in a real Hawaiian place, not a tourist luau. Combine with a scenic drive up Kamehameha Hwy.
